execution.commit_title: docs: clarify DNS TXT record permanence for SSO domain verification
execution.pr_title: docs: clarify DNS TXT record permanence for SSO domain verification
execution.pr_body:
## Summary
Adds a clarifying note to the Domain Verification section of the Enterprise SSO Authentication guide, stating that the DNS TXT record must remain in DNS configuration for as long as SSO is active.

## User Request
"For the Domain Verification DNS TXT entry; is this a permanent requirement or just a one time requirement when setting up the system?"

## Reason for Change
The existing documentation provides step-by-step instructions for creating the DNS TXT record during SSO setup but omits any guidance on whether the record must remain permanently or can be removed after initial verification. This is a genuine documentation gap — the user cannot determine the record's lifecycle from the current docs, and no information exists elsewhere in the repository.

**Note for reviewers:** The actual re-verification behavior (periodic vs. one-time) is implemented in the Netdata Cloud backend, which is not part of this repository. The note uses conservative wording ("may disable") with a fallback directing users to contact Netdata support. Product team confirmation of the exact behavior is required before merge.

context.user_request: For the Domain Verification DNS TXT entry; is this a permanent requirement or just a one time requirement when setting up the system?

plan_item.summary: Task class: improve_docs — Add a clarifying note to the Domain Verification section in enterprise-sso-authentication.md stating whether the DNS TXT record must remain in place permanently or can be removed after initial verification is complete.
plan_item.edit_guidance: In the Domain Verification subsection (lines 31-48), after the DNS record specifications table and before the '### SSO Provider Configuration' heading, add a short paragraph or callout clarifying the permanence of the DNS TXT record. Recommended wording: a note block (using `:::note` or `:::info` syntax) stating something like: 'The DNS TXT record must remain in your DNS configuration for as long as SSO is active. Netdata periodically re-verifies domain ownership; removing the record will disable SSO authentication for your space.' Adjust the factual claim based on product team confirmation of whether re-verification is periodic or one-time. If it is truly one-time, instead state: 'The DNS TXT record is only required during the initial verification process. Once verification is complete, you may remove it without affecting SSO functionality.' — The product team should confirm which behavior is correct before finalizing the wording.
acceptance_checks: The Domain Verification section includes an explicit statement about whether the DNS TXT record must remain permanently or can be removed after verification.
The statement uses an appropriate admonition syntax (e.g., `:::note` or `:::info`) consistent with the existing document style.
No changes are made outside the Domain Verification subsection.
The factual claim about permanence/re-verification has been confirmed by the product team before merge.

context.diff:
diff --git a/docs/netdata-cloud/authentication-and-authorization/enterprise-sso-authentication.md b/docs/netdata-cloud/authentication-and-authorization/enterprise-sso-authentication.md
index 64160eb0a..b9b3d1e4a 100644
--- a/docs/netdata-cloud/authentication-and-authorization/enterprise-sso-authentication.md
+++ b/docs/netdata-cloud/authentication-and-authorization/enterprise-sso-authentication.md
@@ -46,6 +46,12 @@ Domain verification is required to establish secure SSO connectivity:
 | **Name/Host/Alias**          | Leave blank or use @ for subdomain           |
 | **TTL (Time to Live)**       | 86400 (or use provider default)              |
 
+:::info
+
+The DNS TXT record must remain in your DNS configuration for as long as SSO is active. Removing the record may disable SSO authentication for your space. If you need to remove the record after initial setup, contact Netdata support to confirm whether your domain verification will remain valid.
+
+:::
+
 ### SSO Provider Configuration
 
 Consult your provider's documentation for detailed instructions.
